category_title: Preschoolers engaging in loud, chaotic, 'trashing' play
minimal_context: The teacher instructs the preschoolers to clean up the dumped toys and get dressed at the end of their play session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: The game is over. It is time to clean up the toys and put your clothes back on.

STUDENT: (Trudging slowly and wearily) I don't know how to put things away.

TUTOR: Just start picking them up.

STUDENT: (Still sitting without clothes on after playing the animal game) I'm hungry.

TUTOR: Please get dressed first.

STUDENT: (Complaining and taking considerable time to get dressed) I need to use the toilet.

options.A: Read targeted social stories (e.g., Cleaning Up and Playing Safely) for the whole group every day before free-play time, making sure the students have time to understand and process them.
options.B: Adopt a 'leaky' and 'viscous' orientation to control, knowing that the escalating unruliness would only begin again, rather than instituting a much more inflexible regime of disciplinary control.
options.C: Review a daily behavior chart, have the student count the positive and negative marks, and talk to them about whether they had an overall positive or negative play session.
options.D: Verbally reiterate and review all rules (supported with pictures using visual symbols) with the children daily at the beginning of the day and before every free-play activity.
